title: Slack Bot Self-Mention Does NOT Trigger Socket Events — Routing Limitation
desc: Critical limitation discovered 2026-04-17: when a Slack bot posts a message containing its own @mention (via chat.postMessage with bot token OR via incoming webhook belonging to the SAME app), Slack does NOT fire app_mention events. wkappbot Eye listens via Socket Mode for app_mention events only, so self-mentions silently fail to route to Claude Desktop. Future sessions trying to auto-trigger Eye routing from GitHub Actions or scheduled scripts WILL hit this wall. This skill documents the workarounds that DO work.
steps: THE WALL: Slack intentionally does not fire app_mention events when the mentioning author is the SAME APP as the mentioned bot. This applies to (a) chat.postMessage with bot xoxb- token, (b) incoming webhooks belonging to the same app, (c) scheduled messages from same app. Eye Socket Mode only subscribes to app_mention events, so self-mentions are invisible to Eye routing.

SYMPTOM: message posts successfully to Slack channel, renders correctly with <@U...> mention shown as @botname in blue, but Eye does nothing. No log entries, no Claude Desktop prompt, no ask response. Complete silent failure.

WORKAROUND 1 (RECOMMENDED): User OAuth token xoxp-. Install app with chat:write under 'User Token Scopes' (not just Bot Token Scopes). Reinstall to workspace. User OAuth Token appears on Install Page. Posts made with xoxp- appear as authored by the USER, not the bot. Eye sees a real user mentioning the bot → app_mention fires → routing works.

WORKAROUND 2: Second Slack app. Create a separate app (call it 'WkAutoBot' or similar) just for posting. It has its own bot_token. When it mentions wkappbot, that's cross-app mention → app_mention fires for wkappbot. More management overhead but no user-consent coupling.
WORKAROUND 3: Real human posts manually. Simplest. User sees briefing data, types '@wkappbot please analyze', gets response. Not automated but works end-to-end for ad-hoc use.

WORKAROUND 4 (BAD): Don't use Slack routing at all. Use local Claude Code with wkappbot MCP directly. Ad-hoc prompt 'please brief me' with MCP tools reading market_data + retrospective DB. No Slack intermediary. Manual trigger but no OAuth setup.

VERIFICATION TEST: After any Slack integration change, post a test message and check both (a) chat.postMessage returns ok=true with valid ts, AND (b) Eye logs show a routing attempt within 5 seconds. If (a) succeeds but (b) is silent, you hit the self-mention wall.

CODE PATTERN for xoxp- routing: payload = {'channel': C, 'text': msg, 'as_user': True, 'link_names': True}; post with Authorization: Bearer ${xoxp_token}. Message appears from the user who installed the app. scripts/post_briefing_request.py in kiexpert/WkAutoQuant implements this as mode='user'.
HOW TO FIND BOT user_id: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer $bot_token' https://slack.com/api/auth.test. Returns user_id field. Use that as <@user_id> in message text for mentions.

SIGNS you're hitting this wall: Slack channel history shows your message, ts is valid, Korean/emojis render correctly, mention is formatted blue, but Claude Desktop shows nothing, Eye log shows no app_mention event for that ts. Don't debug the message format — it's the sender identity that's the problem.
author: claude
